text: what about a CVT? like an adjustable universal pulley that changes it's diameter based on it's circular velocity to drive a belt which turns the chosen motor. It might be tricky to tune but it will transmit the right amount of speed and torque to the motor based on the wind speed.

I did create a generator via utube instructions, wiring change + magnets, from a ceiling fan, which via hand held drill and inexpensive, Neiko digital tach, created 25-35vac @ ~90rpm.  Worthwhile or just a fun experiment?  I don't know

author: @hubrisnaut
text: With magnets at the rim and an arrangement of coils, you could close the circuit on the coils individually depending on wind speed (available torque). Low wind speed, fewer coils in the charging circuit. High wind speed, more coils in the charging circuit. There are a number of ways to accomplish this. For instance, older motors used literal rotating weights to deactivate starter windings, The concept could be reversed to activate coils in the circuit. A more "modern" way would be to use the "pulse" frequency of induction to regulate individual circuit coils.  The frequency of induction would "close" or activate individual coils.

replies.author: @hubrisnaut
replies.text: Also, something to consider to help reduce induction "cogging" is magnet field direction. Magnets can be arranged at an angle to the direction of rotation.

It's really sad and I am deeply afraid that gullible people will follow this clown down his "research" path completely and unnecessarily waste their time and money  . The science and more importantly the equations are well known , many for over 100 years , there is NO mystery in wind turbines at all . Stop it !!

author: @edkoetsier339
text: I currently have 3 big axial flux turbines that I made and have wooden blades that I knock up in about 30 mins. the blades last about 18 months. All my my turbines turbines have a hinge arrangement that tilts the turbine out of the wind when it gets to ~85 km/h. It's a trial and error to set up first. It works well.

author: @undernetjack
text: Was wondering if Rob ever thought of using the "strong adhesive magnetic tape" kind of magnet in long way round strips , like the magnet wire going long way round the hub. Hope I got the point across?

replies.author: @JacoKruger.
replies.text: Think you need north south north south, spaces in between magnets, those tape ones have north one side and south other side

replies.author: @undernetjack
replies.text: @JacoKruger. Right, so in stacks of two inch pieces of the tape, flipping polarity as you work the way around. They sell the tape with no adhesive as well so you can use your own to suit. My thinking is with the thinner magnet tape one might be able to close the distance between hub and flywheel one layer at a time, minding polarity, having only small or no gaps between pieces. Let's say the gap between hub and flywheel at the closest approach is 7 mm, then you could use stacks of 6 of the 1 mm thick tape. It would have to go onto the flywheel edge, closest to the hub with the coil. Does this better explain?

author: @tiredironrepair
text: I took a que from you and tested the output from a treadmill motor for a generator against using 6 magnets placed on the perimeter the bicycle wheel I'm building off of and was amazed at just how much more power I was able to get with a simple coil around steel pick up I salvaged from a heat and massage pad you would lay on top of. My 2.5   hp motor made 2-3 volts dc out where as the coil placed close to 6 ceramic magnets produced 5-6 ac at the same speed. I would have thought not. Now I need to rectify it and see if I still have better output. Thanks for your uploads

author: @TheKenWiley
text: I noticed at the end of the video you're holding the large gear wheel next to the rose fan.  If you used the large gear wheel as the base for the rose fan you'd eliminate 1 piece and (with a decent material) stabilize and strengthen the rose fan... maybe?

replies.author: @carmichaelmoritz8662
replies.text: the only way is using an enclosed gear box that way you get the longest life span.

replies.author: @TheKenWiley
replies.text: @carmichaelmoritz8662 An enclosed gear box would eliminate using the gear as the base of the fan.  Exposed gears have been used in rural and industrial applications for decades. How long is the fan going to be viable?  Enclose the generator at the small gear.

replies.author: @carmichaelmoritz8662
replies.text: @TheKenWiley exposed gears wear out way quicker.   the fan would be viable for many many lifetimes. and using a belt drive would use up resources due to the tension.

replies.author: @TheKenWiley
replies.text: @carmichaelmoritz8662 I'll tell my 80 year old "politician carriage" that it should be worn out now.  Yes, I've repaired and replaced parts, but it's still running, long after many enclosed mechanical pieces of equipment, and hundreds of commercial "ecological" wind turbines containing millions of dollars of enclosed everything.  I'm looking at KISS engineering.   If it works I'll consider putting a cover and an oiler on the gear.

replies.author: @TheKenWiley
replies.text: @carmichaelmoritz8662 I would never use belt drive.  Too fussy, to much maintenance!  Besides, living more than 1 lifetime at a time burns up lifetimes too fast.  Advancing technology will kill the generator long before it wears out.

author: @cyril3935
text:
Hi from France !
Dear Robert,

Some years ago, I have dismantled a speed box used in Peugeot's 306 1.4 petrol car. In the second shaft you have a very interesting component, supplied with precision ground bearings.
And some speed boxes are using synchronizers made of steel. Only a little punch, with a linear actuator or via the fork can clutch the rose to the desired generator.

This system stays in a oil bath. But regarding your excellent ways to coat iron-based metals, maybe you have an interesting path here.

It will surely demands a consequent lathe, bigger than the one you are currently using in order to simplify the second shaft. The contamination of gears with the abrasive wheel's debris should be avoided with proper protections. Most of the bearings are set with liquid nitrogen, no hydraulic press can be useful here.

And some generators used electric scooters car provide up to 750 watts, because they are using 3-phase coils. The only issue is the Park's transformation, via the 3 embedded sensors which are helping the synchronize the phase's signals regarding the position of the shaft.

Best regards, keep up this interesting path !

author: @Homesteading101
text:
Hi Robert,  I watch your videos with interest and being a mechanic I know that Air and fluid behave quite a lot alike.
Do you know how an automatic transmission torque converter works?  It multiplies torque by harnessing the incoming fluid force.  They are somewhat inefficient, but may have a benefit of the torque multiplication in very low speed wind. It would be interesting to explore.
